tex:
The bar chart shows the number of goals scored by a team in each of 5 months.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) In February, 12 goals are scored.
\newline

 Complete the bar chart.
\newline

(b) How many more goals were scored in January than in October?

(b)Find the range of the number of goals scored.
\newline

(c)
\begin{subpartsaliged}
The team shop is open from 09 00 to 17 15 on Monday to Friday only.
\newline

 Work out how long the shop is open each week.
\newline
 Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

Bruno buys a shirt for \$35 and a scarf for \$12.25 .
\newline
 He pays with a \$50 note.
\newline

 Work out how much change he receives.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(d)
**Ticket prices**

\begin{tabular}{ll}
Adult & \$35 \\
Child & \$20 \\
Senior & \$25 \\
\end{tabular}
\begin{subpartsaliged}
Calculate the cost of 15 adult tickets, 7 child tickets and 3 senior tickets.
\newline

Calculate the percentage of these tickets that are senior tickets.
\newline

\end{subpartsaliged}

(e)A game starts at 15 00.
\newline
 The team plays for 90 minutes.
\newline
 There is also a break of 15 minutes.
\newline

 Find the time the game ends.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:

(f) $\sqrt[3]{80}$

(g) Ranjit buys some plants and sells $\frac{5}{11}$ of them.
\newline
 He sells 190 plants.
\newline

 Work out how many plants he buys.
\newline

\newline
 $15x^3y - 3x$

(i) Make $n$ the subject of the formula $V = 3n + t$.
\newline

(j) $7^{15} \div 7^x = 7^9$

 Find the value of $x$.
\newline

qtype: graphs of quadratic functions, completing table of values, drawing and solving graphically
tex:
(a) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 4x - 2$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c||c|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
$x$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ & $4$ & $5$ \\
\hline
$y$ &  & $3$ & $-2$ & $-5$ &  & $-5$ & $-2$ & $3$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 4x - 2$ for $-2 \leqslant x \leqslant 5$.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 4x - 2 = 0$.
\newline

content.content:

\newline

tex:
Heidi records the colour of each of 500 cars crossing a bridge.
\newline
 The pie chart shows some of this information.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) How many cars are red?

(b) 35 cars are grey.
\newline

 Show, by calculation, that the sector angle for grey is $25.2^\circ$.
\newline

(c) 175 cars are white and 150 cars are black.
\newline

 Complete the pie chart to show this information.
\newline

(d) Find the probability that a car chosen at random is not grey.
\newline
 Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

(e) Another 320 cars cross the bridge.
\newline

 How many of these 320 cars are expected to be white?

(f) Heidi also records the number of people in each car crossing the bridge for one hour.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Number of people & Frequency \\
\hline
1 & 20 \\
2 & 6 \\
3 & 0 \\
4 & 15 \\
5 & 8 \\
6 & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
 Calculate the mean.
\newline

\newline

\newline

tex:
Elize, Lily and Marco start a business.
\newline

(a) Elize invests \$5000.
\newline
 Lily invests \$8000.
\newline
 Marco invests \$3000.
\newline

 After one year they make a profit of \$40~000.
\newline
 They share this profit in the ratio of their investments.
\newline

 Work out how much they each receive.
\newline

(b) (i) (c) Lily buys 20 rolls of ribbon.
\newline
 [number] are red, [number] are blue, [number] are yellow and [number] are pink.
\newline
 A roll of ribbon is chosen at random.
\newline

 On the probability scale, draw an arrow (\raisebox{-0.5ex}{\tikz}) to show the probability that this roll is

 (a) yellow

 (b) not red

 (c) green.
\newline

(d) The length, $l$ m, of a roll of ribbon is 120 m, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

 Complete this statement about the value of $l$.
\newline

(e) Elize buys some picture frames.
\newline
 The frames cost \$5.80 each in New York and 4.50 euros each in Paris.
\newline
 The exchange rate is 1 euro = \$[1.3].
\newline

 Calculate the difference in the cost in euros.
\newline
 Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

(f) Elize buys a framed picture.
\newline

(i) (g) %\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

 The picture is a circle with diameter 18 cm.
\newline
 The frame is a square of side length 18 cm.
\newline

 Calculate the shaded area.
\newline

(h) Elize buys the framed picture for \$12.50 .
\newline
 She sells the framed picture for \$20.25 .
\newline

 Calculate the percentage profit.
\newline

content.content:
Elize invests \$5000.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Lily invests \$8000.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Marco invests \$3000.
\newline

\noindent \hspace{1em}After one year they make a profit of \$40~000.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}They share this profit in the ratio of their investments.
\newline

\noindent \hspace{1em}Work out how much they each receive.
\newline

The length, $l$ m, of a roll of ribbon is 120 m, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

\noindent \hspace{1em}Complete this statement about the value of $l$.
\newline

Elize buys some picture frames.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}The frames cost \$5.80 each in New York and 4.50 euros each in Paris.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}The exchange rate is 1 euro = \$[1.3].
\newline

\noindent \hspace{1em}Calculate the difference in the cost in euros.
\newline
\noindent \hspace{1em}Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

Elize buys a framed picture.
\newline

\noindent \hspace{1em}The picture is a circle with diameter 18 cm.

\noindent \hspace{1em}The frame is a square of side length 18 cm.

\noindent \hspace{1em}Calculate the shaded area.
\newline

Elize buys the framed picture for \$12.50 .

tex:
Triangles A, B and C are shown on the grid.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B.
\newline

(b) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle C.
\newline

(c) On the grid, translate triangle A by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 6 \\ -4 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

(d) On the grid, reflect triangle A in the line $y = -2$.
\newline

content.content:
Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B.
\newline

Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle C.
\newline

On the grid, translate triangle A by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} 6 \\ -4 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

On the grid, reflect triangle A in the line $y = -2$.
\newline
